title: SOPHIAS AICoFe assessments schema
description: Rubric-based assessment records collected through the AICoFe system for a given presenter and rubric item. Each record contains the evaluated student identifier, rubric item and section identifiers, evaluator identifiers, numerical scores, and free-text comments from peer evaluators, the teacher, and the presenter self-assessment.

id_evaluado.description: Internal AICoFe identifier of the evaluated student, corresponding to the presenter being assessed.

id_item.description: Internal identifier of the rubric item being assessed, such as voice and tone, body language, eye contact, slide design, or mastery of the topic.

id_seccion.description: Internal identifier of the rubric section to which the item belongs, such as content, design, or presenter performance.

id_evaluador1.description: Internal AICoFe identifier of the first peer evaluator assigned to assess the presenter.

id_evaluador2.description: Internal AICoFe identifier of the second peer evaluator assigned to assess the presenter.

nota_evaluador1.description: Numerical score assigned by the first peer evaluator to the evaluated student for the corresponding rubric item.

nota_evaluador2.description: Numerical score assigned by the second peer evaluator to the evaluated student for the corresponding rubric item.

nota_profesor.description: Numerical score assigned by the teacher to the evaluated student for the corresponding rubric item.

nota_evaluado.description: Numerical self-assessment score assigned by the evaluated student for the corresponding rubric item.

observaciones_evaluador1.description: Free-text comment written by the first peer evaluator for the corresponding rubric item.

observaciones_evaluador2.description: Free-text comment written by the second peer evaluator for the corresponding rubric item.

observaciones_profesor.description: Free-text comment written by the teacher for the corresponding rubric item.

observaciones_evaluado.description: Free-text self-assessment comment written by the evaluated student for the corresponding rubric item.
